assertions: Before delegating work, the flow confirms `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` exists and, if that file were absent, it would halt with only the initialise-first guidance and skip spawning `zoto-spec-executor`.
Upon success it spawns `zoto-spec-executor`, forwards the empty `$ARGUMENTS`, and targets the newest spec directory beneath the configured specs directory root.
The executor loads the discovered spec index and validates manifest integrity so referenced artefacts exist, agent assignments honour metadata, and dependency ordering forbids skipping prerequisites.
It surfaces an execution summary covering phases, subtask totals, and per-subtask assignments through structured `askQuestion` prior to spawning workers.
Once approved it walks phases sequentially, spawning at most four concurrent manifest-named subagents per batch without rewriting agent identifiers.
After every subtask a separate `zoto-spec-judge` pass must verify deliverables and definition-of-done items rather than trusting the originating executor transcript alone.
It keeps targeted checks during subtasks and defers repo-wide suites until final verification alongside lint passes on altered files before writing the consolidated report.

It backgrounds `pnpm exec tsx plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-aggregator.ts --watch --spec-dir … --repo-root …` from repo root until execution ends, rebuilding status rollups whenever digests detect changes while workers keep sole ownership of their `.status.(md|yml)` artefacts.

It writes `execution-report-[feature-slug]-[yyyymmdd].md` beside the index under the chosen spec folder capturing per-subtask outcomes, verdicts, remediation notes, lint or test deltas, and recorded file mutations.
It replays structured `askQuestion` after presenting that report and only transitions the spec completion flag after operator approval acknowledging the artefacts.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases stress manifest-driven execution with dual askQuestion gates, adversarial judging, durable reporting, resume behaviour, failure remediation choices, and the split between live-reloaded versus restart-only configuration keys.

cases.expected_output: A controlled run that aborts cleanly when initialise prerequisites are absent, otherwise walks approval, phased dispatch, hostile verification, aggregator telemetry, hardened quality checks, and finishes with durable reporting plus closure gating.

assertions: Directory arguments resolve internally to the `spec-*.md` index beneath that folder so execution mirrors the richer paths without handing the Markdown file explicitly.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases stress manifest-driven execution with dual askQuestion gates, adversarial judging, durable reporting, resume behaviour, failure remediation choices, and the split between live-reloaded versus restart-only configuration keys.

cases.expected_output: Executor treats the supplied directory exactly like palette usage rooted at the specs tree and drives the documented workflow unchanged.

assertions: Pointing directly at the Markdown index honours that file verbatim so nested layouts or unconventional folders still execute reliably.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases stress manifest-driven execution with dual askQuestion gates, adversarial judging, durable reporting, resume behaviour, failure remediation choices, and the split between live-reloaded versus restart-only configuration keys.

cases.expected_output: Planner loads the anchored index straight away rather than probing neighbours for substitutes.

assertions: The `--resume` mode rehydrates persisted manifest checkpoints, skips checklist items already marked complete, and schedules only the earliest incomplete subtasks without duplicating succeeded phases.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases stress manifest-driven execution with dual askQuestion gates, adversarial judging, durable reporting, resume behaviour, failure remediation choices, and the split between live-reloaded versus restart-only configuration keys.

cases.expected_output: Continuation path replays manifests from disk respecting stored progress snapshots.

assertions: After a Failure-class outcome from execution or hostile verification it issues structured `askQuestion` branching across retry skip and abort remedies before advancing.
Selecting abort honours the refusal path leaving later subtasks untouched and withholding final completion signalling.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases stress manifest-driven execution with dual askQuestion gates, adversarial judging, durable reporting, resume behaviour, failure remediation choices, and the split between live-reloaded versus restart-only configuration keys.

cases.expected_output: Demonstrates the documented failure safeguards with observable operator-mediated halt behaviour.
follow_ups: When structured remediation prompts appear following a Failed judge verdict, choose abort so the orchestration freezes instead of spawning more subtasks.

assertions: Mutations under `subagents.*.tokenBudget`, `subagents.*.model`, aggregator cadence knobs, aggregator enable switches, or `spec.parallelLimit` propagate on subsequent spawns without restarting the overarching command invocation.
Edits targeting `specsDir`, `unitOfWork`, `workDir`, `hooks.*`, or `extensions.*` stay latent until `/z-spec-execute` launches again intentionally.

primitive_analysis.summary: These cases stress manifest-driven execution with dual askQuestion gates, adversarial judging, durable reporting, resume behaviour, failure remediation choices, and the split between live-reloaded versus restart-only configuration keys.

cases.expected_output: Clear separation showing hot knobs versus knobs that mandate a freshly issued command entry.
